summary: Markets closed sharply lower on Friday as investors digested Powell's final day as Fed chair and rising inflation concerns. The S&P 500 shed 1.24% to 7,408.50, while the Nasdaq dropped 1.54% amid a tech selloff led by Intel, AMD, and Micron. Semiconductor stocks fell despite recent strength, as the two-day Trump-Xi summit ended without major policy breakthroughs, leaving traders disappointed. On May 15, Jerome Powell concluded his eight-year tenure as Fed chair after the FOMC held rates steady at 3.5-3.75%, with four dissenting votes marking the most divided decision since 1992. The statement cited Middle East turmoil as a key uncertainty driver, while elevated oil prices continue to fuel inflation fears. In crypto, Bitcoin slid below $79,000 as liquidations topped $500 million and ETF outflows snapped a six-week inflow streak. The Senate advanced the CLARITY Act, briefly lifting XRP 5%, though gains faded on broader risk-off sentiment.

signals.title: Fed Holds Rates, Divided FOMC Signals Uncertain Path Forward
signals.desc: Four Fed officials dissented in the most divided vote since 1992, with the FOMC holding rates at 3.5-3.75% and citing Middle East uncertainty as inflation risks mount.

signals.title: Stock Selloff Extends as Trump-Xi Summit Disappoints
signals.desc: Markets fell over 1% Friday as the two-day Trump-Xi talks ended without breakthroughs, triggering profit-taking in tech stocks that had rallied sharply in prior sessions.

signals.title: Bitcoin Liquidations Hit $500M as BTC Slides to $78K
signals.desc: Crypto longs suffered a liquidation cascade as Bitcoin fell below $79,000 and ETF outflows snapped a six-week inflow streak amid global bond selloff pressures.

signals.title: Surging Oil Prices Threaten Markets Amid Iran War Escalation
signals.desc: Brent crude hit a four-year high near $119.50 as Middle East conflict intensifies, raising inflation concerns and weighing on equity valuations.

signals.title: Nvidia Earnings May 20 Could Drive Tech Rebound
signals.desc: Wall Street expects Nvidia to report 77% revenue growth to $78B for fiscal Q1, with strong AI demand potentially offering direction for tech stocks next week.

verdict: Risk-off sentiment dominates as Fed division, oil spike, and crypto outflows signal caution ahead of key Nvidia earnings next week.
